English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Betty Bone Schiess papers
Schiess, Betty Bone.
4468
15.2 cubic feet. 15.2 cubic feet.
1 mapcase folders. 1 mapcase folders.
1965-2015. Correspondence, chronological and subject files, clippings, schedules, photographs, audio and video cassettes, biographical information, and legal documents. Correspondence, litigation files, documents, clippings, photographs, audio and video cassettes concerning the various causes and organizations with which Reverend Schiess was concerned, particularly the ordination of women and feminist issues. Collection material in English 2093727
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
May 2001
EAD encoding:
Daniel Keeley, September 2002
Date Modified:
Marcie Farwell, August 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Episcopal priest.
Betty Bone Schiess graduated from the University of Cincinnati in 1945. She received an M.A. from Syracuse University in 1947 and a Master of Divinity in 1972 from the Rochester Center for Theological Studies. In 1972, she was ordained a deacon of the Episcopal Church in the United States, and ordained as a priest in 1974. The ordination in 1974 in Philadelphia of eleven women by four inactive priests was deemed "irregular" in that it was done without approval of the General Convention of the Episcopal Church. Subsequently, Schiess and her attorney Constance Cook began a law suit against the Bishop of the Diocese of Central New York, Ned Cole, charging him with sex discrimination for refusing to recognize her ordination and preventing her from assuming the position of a parish priest. The suit was discontinued when the General Convention in 1975 approved the ordination of women to the priesthood in the Episcopal Church.
Reverend Schiess served as curate at Grace Episcopal Church in Baldwinsville, N.Y. in 1972-1973. From 1973 to 1984, she was Executive Director, Mizpah Educational and Cultural Center for the Aging in Syracuse; Priest Associate, Grace Episcopal Church, Syracuse; Chaplain, Syracuse University; and Acting Chaplain, Cornell University. She was installed as rector of Grace Church, Mexico, N.Y., in 1984 and became Advisor to Women in Mission and Ministry, Episcopal Church in the United States in 1987.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, litigation files, documents, clippings, photographs, audio and video cassettes concerning the various causes and organizations with which Reverend Schiess was concerned, particularly the ordination of women and feminist issues. Organizations include the International Association of Women Ministers, the Episcopal Society for the Examination of Feminist Issues, the National Organization for Women, and the Movement for the Ordination of Women. Much of the material pertains to the July 1974 ordination of the "Philadelphia 11" and to the Lambeth Conference (England) and the General Convention of the Episcopal Church.
Files, 1889-1991, compiled by Lois M. Black relating to the Episcopal Society of the Examination of Feminist Issues and other topics relating to women's ordination. Includes a nomination submission for the National Women's Hall of Fame, 1994.
Book: Why Me Lord?; typescript paper "Goodbye to All That" explaining her current feelings about the Episcopal Church and women's issues; and cassette tape - New Zealand Public Radio, 1993. Annotated listing of the people who helped her. Copy of "The Little Yankee, From Riches to Rags: Letters of Mary Ann Corlis, 1815-1850," published letters of Betty Bone Schiess's ancestor, Mary Ann Corlis. Typescript notes about people who influenced her, titled "secularism, although still restrained by religious authorities."
Items relating to the 2014 Dewey-Heyward Lectures and EDS Women's Leadership Forum at the Episcopal Divinity School; and program for the 146th Convention of the Episcopal Diocese of Central New York, 2014.Also includes DVD and program for a celebration of Karen DeCrow by the Central New York Women's Bar Association, April 2015
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence and Other Files Boxes 1-3, 6-9, 11, 12, 14-18
Series II. Audio Cassettes Box 10, 15
Series III. Magnetic Stripe Film Box 10
Series IV. Video Cassettes/DVD's Box 10, 18
Series V. Tape Recordings Box 10
Series VI. Film Box 10
Series VII. Photographs Box 11
Series VIII. Connie Cook Litigation Files Box 4, 5, 13
Cite As:
Betty Bone Schiess papers, 1965-1991. #4468. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Syracuse (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs. Feminism -- Religious aspects -- Christianity. Religious institutions -- New York (State) -- Mexico. Religious institutions -- New York (State) -- Baldwinsville. Religious institutions -- New York (State) -- Syracuse. Women priests. Ordination of women. Women clergy. Cole, Ned. Black, Lois M. Corlis, Mary Ann. Episcopal Church. General Convention Episcopal Church. Diocese of Central New York Grace Episcopal Church (Baldwinsville, N.Y.) Mizpah Educational and Cultural Center for the Aging (Syracuse, N.Y.) Grace Episcopal Church (Syracuse, N.Y.) Syracuse University -- : Religion. Cornell University -- : Religion. Episcopal Church. Women in Mission and Ministry International Association of Women Ministers National Organization for Women Lambeth Conference Grace Church (Mexico, N.Y.) Episcopal Society for the Examination of Feminist Issues

Kennedy138Ordinations - blacks139Ordination - church documents1310Ordination/custom - Catholic1311Civil intervention in church affairs1312Church/state - discrimination; aid to school1313Church/state - equal protection1314Church/state - general1315Church/state - religious sects1316Church/state - tax exemption1317women and ordination - general1318Regularization/completion1319McClure1320Serbian Church decision - hierarchy1321Validity - bishops1322Validity - church decisions and documents1323Validity - theologians1324Copies13252. Betty Bone Schiess is a priest of the Episcopal Church13263. Defendant diocese is a corporation13274. Defendant diocese is engaged in interstate commerce and employs more than 15 people13286. Defendant Cole acts as an employment agency13297. Betty Bone Schiess was offered employment by Grace episcopal Church13308. Defendant Cole refused to license Betty Bone Schiess because she's female13319. Betty Bone Schiess Applied to Defendant Cole for employment in parishes where the Defendant acts as an employment agent133210. Bishop Cole Admits that Betty Bone Schiess is qualified in all respects except sex133311. The constitution/canon of the Episcopal Church do NOT prohibit women priests133412. Defendant Cole needs no more authority to license Betty Bone Schiess133514. Bishops conferred so as not to allow the women to function133615. These meetings constituted a conspiracy133716. The bishops prevented an ecclesiastical trial133817. Defendant Cole prevented the filing of presentments against Betty Bone Schiess that might have produced an ecclesiastical trial133918. These acts constitute a conspiracy134019. Betty Bone Schiess filed a timely complaint with the New York State Human Rights Division134120. Betty Bone Schiess filed a timely complaint with EEOC134221. A notice of Betty Bone Schiess's right to sue was issued by EEOC1343#1 client - research - separation of church and state1344#1 client -research - women in religion1345#1 client - CEC working papers1346#1 client -expenditures and finances1347-48#1 client - duplicates1349#1 client - press clippings - 1974, relating to ordination of women and Schiess1350#1 client - staff - working papers (duplicates)1351#1 client - research - ordination validity1352Answer1353Northern District rules and info1354Complaint drafts - old1355#1 client1356General convention19761357 ======= Series I. Women and (Religion) Episcopal Liturgy1972-7456-7Correspondence197658-9#1 client - New York Human Rights510Schiess litigation, C-1511#1 client - U.S. EEOC512Convention1976513Fundraising131Validity - other priests and friends132Complaint - correspondence133Support Materials134News releases135Newsletter - WON136Inter-office memos137Grace C. Kennedy138Ordinations - blacks139Ordination - church documents1310Ordination/custom - Catholic1311Civil intervention in church affairs1312Church/state - discrimination; aid to school1313Church/state - equal protection1314Church/state - general1315Church/state - religious sects1316Church/state - tax exemption1317women and ordination - general1318Regularization/completion1319McClure1320Serbian Church decision - hierarchy1321Validity - bishops1322Validity - church decisions and documents1323Validity - theologians1324Copies13252. Betty Bone Schiess is a priest of the Episcopal Church13263. Defendant diocese is a corporation13274. Defendant diocese is engaged in interstate commerce and employs more than 15 people13286. Defendant Cole acts as an employment agency13297. Betty Bone Schiess was offered employment by Grace episcopal Church13308. Defendant Cole refused to license Betty Bone Schiess because she's female13319. Betty Bone Schiess Applied to Defendant Cole for employment in parishes where the Defendant acts as an employment agent133210. Bishop Cole Admits that Betty Bone Schiess is qualified in all respects except sex133311. The constitution/canon of the Episcopal Church do NOT prohibit women priests133412. Defendant Cole needs no more authority to license Betty Bone Schiess133514. Bishops conferred so as not to allow the women to function133615. These meetings constituted a conspiracy133716. The bishops prevented an ecclesiastical trial133817. Defendant Cole prevented the filing of presentments against Betty Bone Schiess that might have produced an ecclesiastical trial133918. These acts constitute a conspiracy134019. Betty Bone Schiess filed a timely complaint with the New York State Human Rights Division134120. Betty Bone Schiess filed a timely complaint with EEOC134221. A notice of Betty Bone Schiess's right to sue was issued by EEOC1343#1 client - research - separation of church and state1344#1 client -research - women in religion1345#1 client - CEC working papers1346#1 client -expenditures and finances1347-48#1 client - duplicates1349#1 client - press clippings - 1974, relating to ordination of women and Schiess1350#1 client - staff - working papers (duplicates)1351#1 client - research - ordination validity1352Answer1353Northern District rules and info1354Complaint drafts - old1355#1 client1356General convention19761357 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
